channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Will Olivia Rodrigo Be Able to Beat Out Ella Langley for the Hot 100’s No. 1 Spot Next Week?
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to this week's music update. We're taking a look at the contenders for the top spot on the Billboard Hot 100 chart. This week, we're pitting Olivia Rodrigo's new single "Drop Dead" against Ella Langley's current number one hit "Choosin' Texas". Let's dive in.
[Soft music transitions in the background]
Host: Olivia Rodrigo has a history of debuting at number one on the Billboard Hot 100 with her new album's lead singles. Her breakout hit "Drivers License" topped the chart in 2021, and her sophomore effort "Vampire" did the same two years later. Now, she's looking to make it three-for-three with "Drop Dead", the first single from her upcoming album "You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love".
[Music transitions to a clip of "Drop Dead"]
Host: "Drop Dead" has already gotten off to a strong start, topping Spotify's Daily Top Songs USA chart and reaching number two on Apple Music's real-time chart. But to take the top spot from Ella Langley, Rodrigo will need to rely on sales. With multiple music videos, cover variants, and different edits of the song available, Rodrigo is giving fans plenty of options to buy and stream her music.
[Music transitions back to the host]
Host: Rodrigo's team is pulling out all the stops to promote the single, with digital and physical releases available, including a sold-out vinyl edition. It's a campaign reminiscent of those for megastars like Taylor Swift and BTS. Will it be enough to take the top spot? We'll have to wait and see.
[Outro music plays]
Host: That's all for this week. Tune in next week to see if Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" can take the top spot on the Billboard Hot 100 chart. Thanks for listening.
